channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump White House celebrates release of two Americans freed in a swap with Taliban brokered by Biden
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JON GAMBRELL and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russia warned President Trump on Tuesday against seizing the Panama Canal, after he reiterated his intent to take control of the strategic waterway in his second inaugural address Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Suspected settlers attacked Palestinian villages hours before Trump rescinded Biden sanctions
item.description: Shortly after suspected Jewish settlers stormed Palestinian villages in the occupied West Bank, U.S. President Donald Trump canceled sanctions against Israelis accused of violence in the territory.
item.dc:creator: Aref Tufaha And Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 66 dead after fire erupts at popular ski hotel in Turkey
item.description: At least 66 people died and 51 others became injured in a fire that broke out at a Turkish ski resort hotel on Tuesday. Over 200 guests were staying at the hotel when the disaster struck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli military launches large-scale counterterrorism operation in West Bank city
item.description: The Israel Defense Forces announced an operation in the West Bank city of Jenin, which is a stronghold for terror organizations backed by Iran.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Same-sex marriages will soon become legal in Thailand after historic law
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — They have been in a committed relationship for more than 13 years, and even had a wedding in 2019. Since then, Danaya Phonphayung and Sunma Piamboon, both women, have considered themselves a married couple, even if same-sex marriages were not legally recognized.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Landslides and flash floods on Indonesia’s Java island leave 17 dead and 8 missing
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian rescuers recovered the bodies of at least 17 people who were swept away in flash floods or buried under tons of mud and rocks that hit hilly villages on the country’s main island of Java, officials said Tuesday. Eight people were missing.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Strong earthquake in Taiwan injures 27 and causes scattered damage
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A 6.4-magnitude earthquake struck southern Taiwan early Tuesday, leaving 27 people with minor injuries and some reported damage. The quake hit at 12:17 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia's president will declare an emergency over deadly ELN attacks in the northeast
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro said Monday that he will declare a state of emergency over the guerrilla attacks in the northeast that have killed dozens of people and forced thousands to flee their homes.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Davos 2025 reacts to Trump tack, EU chief leads defense against climate change
item.description: President Donald Trump has given everyone at Davos something to talk about with his actions on the first day of his second term.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump wants to pull the US out of the World Health Organization again. Here's what may happen next
item.description: President Donald Trump has used one of the flurry of executive actions that he issued on his first day back in the White House to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from the World Health Organization for the second time in less than five years.
item.dc:creator: Maria Cheng And Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Panama, familiar with US intervention, bristles at Trump's comments on canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — U.S. President Donald Trump’s insistence Monday that he wants to have the Panama Canal back under U.S. control fed nationalist sentiment and worry in Panama, home to the critical trade route and a country familiar with U.S. military intervention.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico defends sovereignty as US seeks to label cartels as terrorists
item.description: Mexico President Claudia Sheinbaum says President Donald Trump’s executive order moving toward designating drug cartels as foreign terrorist organizations would only impact Mexico if there’s close coordination between the two governments.
item.dc:creator: Megan Janetsky, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump signs executive order pausing foreign assistance for 3 months
item.description: President Trump on Monday signed an executive order suspending any new U.S. foreign development assistance for three months, and directed a review of foreign assistance programs to ensure they line up with his America First foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli president calls Trump a 'true friend' on Inauguration Day, praises his work to release hostages
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog is praising Donald Trump as a "true friend" of Israel, thanking him for his "commitment to bringing all our hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Freed Israeli hostage speaks out for first time, says she has 'returned to my life' following Hamas captivity
item.description: Emily Damari, one of the first hostages freed by Hamas in the cease-fire agreement, is speaking out for the first time following her release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 8 people die in a fire at a home for the elderly in Serbia, officials say
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Eight people died Monday in a fire at a home for older people on the outskirts of the Serbian capital, Belgrade, that is suspected of being set by a resident, officials said. Seven others were injured in the blaze that erupted around 3:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners after Hamas frees 3 Israeli hostages
item.description: Israel has freed 90 Palestinian prisoners and detainees. The release came early Monday, more than seven hours after three Israeli hostages released from Hamas captivity in Gaza returned to Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Graham calls for the 'decimation' of Iranian nuclear program after ceasefire
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) on Sunday called for the “decimation” of the Iranian nuclear program in the wake of a new ceasefire in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Trump wants to visit China, strengthen relationship: Report
item.description: The president-elect has vowed to impose tariffs and other mechanisms against China upon his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: German ambassador warns Trump will 'undermine' democratic principles with 'maximum disruption' agenda: report
item.description: In a confidential briefing document, Germany's ambassador to the United States Andreas Michaelis warned that Donald Trump will "undermine" democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to expect as Israel-Hamas cease-fire goes into effect on Sunday
item.description: The Israeli government approved the deal early Saturday morning for a cease-fire in Gaza and the release of hostages captured after Hamas' terrorist attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Exploring Greenland's majestic history as President-elect Trump shines spotlight on island nation
item.description: Greenland is the world's largest island, but most of it is covered by ice. Its southern tip, however, is sprinkled with picturesque towns and villages that are isolated from each other.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gunman shoots dead 2 Supreme Court judges in Iran's capital before turning gun on himself, state media says
item.description: Two Iranian Supreme Court judges were shot and killed in Tehran on Saturday while a third judge and a bodyguard were wounded, although the motive is unclear.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel, Hamas to go into effect Sunday morning, Qatar says
item.description: The cease-fire between Hamas and Israel is expected to go into effect on Sunday at 8:30 a.m. local time, Qatar’s foreign ministry announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Cabinet approves a deal for a ceasefire in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet approved a deal early Saturday for a ceasefire in Gaza that would release dozens of hostages held there and pause the 15-month war with Hamas, bringing the sides a step closer to ending their deadliest and most destructive fighting ever.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, WAFAA SHURAFA and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ancient Pompeii excavation uncovers lavish private bath complex
item.description: A private bath complex in Pompeii has been unearthed by archaeologists; the baths highlight the wealth of the ancient city before it was destroyed in AD 79.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli security experts argue ‘bad' deal with Hamas, but ‘no other way’ to free hostages
item.description: Security experts analyzing the deal brokered between Israel and Hamas argue it was a 'bad deal' as it could pose security risks for Israel but one that needed to be done to return the hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican influencers reportedly killed for ties to Sinaloa cartel
item.description: Bloodshed between members of the Sinaloa cartel may be common, but the death of influencers is a new development.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Russia, Iran to seal partnership treaty days before Trump takes office
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin and Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian will meet in Moscow to sign a partnership pact as the two nations brace for President-elect Trump's return.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazilian court denies Bolsonaro’s request to travel to Trump’s inauguration
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court has denied former President Jair Bolsonaro's request to have his passport temporarily restored in order to attend the inauguration of U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two Americans to be released from Gaza in first phase of ceasefire deal
item.description: Two Americans, Keith Siegel and Sagui Dekel-Chen, are expected to be released as part of the first phase of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal that emerged Wednesday after being held for 15-months by Hamas since the start of the war in Gaza, according to a senior administration official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Families of hostages grateful but cautious about ceasefire deal
item.description: While some may soon reunite with their loved ones, others face the grim possibility of receiving confirmation of deaths during captivity.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Ceasefire in Gaza begins, Hamas releases hostage names to Israel
item.description: The ceasefire was delayed but will take effect Sunday after Hamas released the list of hostage names to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation
